comment_text.sn3.20:3.2: The concept of the paccekabuddha allows that there were awakened sages in the long periods of time when there is no sambuddha such as Gotama. Both are Buddhas in the sense of being awakened, but they differ in their relation to others. It is sometimes said that they do not teach, but the tradition attributes to them a number of sayings (notably Snp 1.3). They are sometimes said to be “solitary”, but in MN 116, 500 dwell on the same hill, while pacceka Brahmās appear in pairs (SN 6.6:1.3). Compare Bu NP 9:1.36.1, where “independent” funds are set up for “independent” robes (rather than combining them); the same idea occurs at AN 11.16:11.2. Likewise, SN 1.37:1.7 speaks of “independent” verses. Thus the burden of sense in the Pali is that pacceka means “independent’, which here has the sense of not forming a fourfold community, but simply living and practicing independently.
comment_text.sn3.20:3.3: He did not give with his own hands.
comment_text.sn3.20:3.5: Giving to workers is obviously good, but it is not good to regret one’s generosity.
comment_text.sn3.20:4.1: This sequence shows the complexity of deeds and their results.
comment_text.sn3.20:4.4: The regret did not cancel out the good deed, but it took the shine off it.
comment_text.sn3.20:4.5: The results are proportionate to the deeds.
comment_text.sn3.20:4.6: Rebirth in hell was for the murder, while childlessness is because he tried to usurp the inheritance.

root_text.sn3.20:1.1: Atha kho rājā pasenadi kosalo divā divassa yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho rājānaṁ pasenadiṁ kosalaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.20:1.2: “handa kuto nu tvaṁ, mahārāja, āgacchasi divā divassā”ti?
root_text.sn3.20:2.2: Tamahaṁ aputtakaṁ sāpateyyaṁ rājantepuraṁ atiharitvā āgacchāmi.
root_text.sn3.20:2.3: Sataṁ, bhante, satasahassāni hiraññasseva, ko pana vādo rūpiyassa.
root_text.sn3.20:2.4: Tassa kho pana, bhante, seṭṭhissa gahapatissa evarūpo bhattabhogo ahosi—
root_text.sn3.20:3.2: Bhūtapubbaṁ so, mahārāja, seṭṭhi gahapati taggarasikhiṁ nāma paccekasambuddhaṁ piṇḍapātena paṭipādesi.
root_text.sn3.20:3.4: Datvā ca pana pacchā vippaṭisārī ahosi:
root_text.sn3.20:3.5: ‘varametaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ dāsā vā kammakarā vā bhuñjeyyun’ti.
root_text.sn3.20:3.6: Bhātu ca pana ekaputtakaṁ sāpateyyassa kāraṇā jīvitā voropesi.
root_text.sn3.20:4.1: Yaṁ kho so, mahārāja, seṭṭhi gahapati taggarasikhiṁ paccekasambuddhaṁ piṇḍapātena paṭipādesi, tassa kammassa vipākena sattakkhattuṁ sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajji.
root_text.sn3.20:4.2: Tasseva kammassa vipākāvasesena imissāyeva sāvatthiyā sattakkhattuṁ seṭṭhittaṁ kāresi.
root_text.sn3.20:4.3: Yaṁ kho so, mahārāja, seṭṭhi gahapati datvā pacchā vippaṭisārī ahosi:
root_text.sn3.20:4.4: ‘varametaṁ piṇḍapātaṁ dāsā vā kammakarā vā bhuñjeyyun’ti, tassa kammassa vipākena nāssuḷārāya bhattabhogāya cittaṁ namati, nāssuḷārāya vatthabhogāya cittaṁ namati, nāssuḷārāya yānabhogāya cittaṁ namati, nāssuḷārānaṁ pañcannaṁ kāmaguṇānaṁ bhogāya cittaṁ namati.
root_text.sn3.20:4.5: Yaṁ kho so, mahārāja, seṭṭhi gahapati bhātu ca pana ekaputtakaṁ sāpateyyassa kāraṇā jīvitā voropesi, tassa kammassa vipākena bahūni vassāni bahūni vassasatāni bahūni vassasahassāni bahūni vassasatasahassāni niraye paccittha.
root_text.sn3.20:4.6: Tasseva kammassa vipākāvasesena idaṁ sattamaṁ aputtakaṁ sāpateyyaṁ rājakosaṁ paveseti.
root_text.sn3.20:4.7: Tassa kho, mahārāja, seṭṭhissa gahapatissa purāṇañca puññaṁ parikkhīṇaṁ, navañca puññaṁ anupacitaṁ.
root_text.sn3.20:4.8: Ajja pana, mahārāja, seṭṭhi gahapati mahāroruve niraye paccatī”ti.
root_text.sn3.20:4.9: “Evaṁ, bhante, seṭṭhi gahapati mahāroruvaṁ nirayaṁ upapanno”ti.
root_text.sn3.20:4.10: “Evaṁ, mahārāja, seṭṭhi gahapati mahāroruvaṁ nirayaṁ upapanno”ti.

translation_text.sn3.20:1.1: Then King Pasenadi of Kosala went up to the Buddha in the middle of the day … The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn3.20:1.2: “So, great king, where are you coming from in the middle of the day?”
translation_text.sn3.20:2.1: “Sir, here in Sāvatthī a financier householder has passed away.
translation_text.sn3.20:2.2: Since he died childless, I have come after transferring his fortune to the royal compound.
translation_text.sn3.20:2.3: There was ten million in gold coin, not to mention the silver coin.
translation_text.sn3.20:2.4: And yet that financier ate meals of
translation_text.sn3.20:2.5: rough gruel with false black pepper.
translation_text.sn3.20:2.8: He traveled around in a vehicle that was
translation_text.sn3.20:2.9: a dilapidated little cart, holding a leaf as parasol.”
translation_text.sn3.20:3.1: “That’s so true, great king! That’s so true!
translation_text.sn3.20:3.2: Once upon a time, great king, that financier householder provided almsfood on behalf of an Independent Buddha named Tagarasikhī.
translation_text.sn3.20:3.3: He instructed: ‘Give alms to that ascetic,’ before getting up from his seat and leaving.
translation_text.sn3.20:3.4: But after giving he regretted it:
translation_text.sn3.20:3.5: ‘It would have been better to feed the bondservants or workers with that almsfood.’
translation_text.sn3.20:3.6: What’s more, he murdered his brother’s only child for the sake of his fortune.
translation_text.sn3.20:4.1: Because that financier provided Tagarasikhī with almsfood, as a result of that deed he was reborn seven times in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.sn3.20:4.2: And as a residual result of that same deed he held the position of financier seven times right here in Sāvatthī.
translation_text.sn3.20:4.3: But because that financier regretted giving alms,
translation_text.sn3.20:4.4: as a result of that deed his mind didn’t tend to enjoy nice food, clothes, vehicles, or the five refined kinds of sensual stimulation.
translation_text.sn3.20:4.5: And because that financier murdered his brother’s only child for the sake of his fortune, as a result of that deed he burned in hell for many years, for many hundreds, many thousands, many hundreds of thousands of years.
translation_text.sn3.20:4.6: And as a residual result of that same deed, he is childless for the seventh time, his fortune ending up in the royal treasury.
translation_text.sn3.20:4.7: Now the old merit of that financier has been used up, and he hasn’t accumulated new merit.
translation_text.sn3.20:4.8: Today, great king, that financier burns in the Great Hell of Screams.”
translation_text.sn3.20:4.9: “Really, sir, that financier has been reborn in the Great Hell of Screams?”
translation_text.sn3.20:4.11: That is what the Buddha said. …
translation_text.sn3.20:5.2: or whatever other possessions there are;
translation_text.sn3.20:5.4: and those dependent for their livelihood:
translation_text.sn3.20:6.1: you must go on without taking these;
translation_text.sn3.20:6.2: all of them are left behind.
translation_text.sn3.20:7.1: that’s what you can call your own.
translation_text.sn3.20:7.2: That’s what you take when you go.
translation_text.sn3.20:7.4: like a shadow that never leaves.
translation_text.sn3.20:8.1: That’s why you should do good,
translation_text.sn3.20:8.3: The good deeds of sentient beings
translation_text.sn3.20:8.4: support them in the next world.”

reference_text.sn3.20:1.1: ms12S1_706, msdiv131, ndp12.91, pts-vp-pli2ed1.206, sya15.133, vri23.110
